chapter_title: Live trees and other plants; bulbs, roots and the like; cut flowers and ornamental foliage
scope_note: Chapter 6 covers living plants and plant propagation materials for ORNAMENTAL/HORTICULTURAL use, AND cut/prepared plant materials for floral decoration. The chapter is for nursery/florist trade and horticulture — not for food/agricultural produce (those → Ch.07–12). Cut flowers both fresh and dried/preserved for decorative purposes are included.
inclusions: Heading 06.01 — Bulbs, tubers, tuberous roots, corms, crowns and rhizomes, dormant, in growth or in flower; chicory plants and roots other than roots of heading 12.12: tulip bulbs, daffodil bulbs, hyacinth bulbs, dahlia tubers, iris rhizomes, anemone corms, begonia tubers — for ornamental planting. Dormant (dry) or in active growth. Chicory plants including roots (not chicory root for inulin → 12.12)

Heading 06.02 — Other live plants (including their roots), cuttings and slips; mushroom spawn: trees, shrubs, hedges, roses, orchids, potted plants, seedling vegetables (before transplant to food production — when supplied by nursery), rooted cuttings, unrooted slips, grafted plants. Mushroom spawn (mycelium on grain/substrate) → 06.02

Heading 06.03 — Cut flowers and flower buds of a kind suitable for bouquets or for ornamental purposes, fresh, dried, dyed, bleached, impregnated or otherwise prepared: fresh roses, carnations, chrysanthemums, lilies, orchid cut flowers — also dried flowers (lavender, immortelle, lotus pods), bleached/dyed flowers for decoration

Heading 06.04 — Foliage, branches and other parts of plants, without flowers or flower buds, and grasses, mosses and lichens, being goods of a kind suitable for bouquets or for ornamental purposes, fresh, dried, dyed, bleached, impregnated or otherwise prepared: fresh foliage (ferns, eucalyptus, ruscus), dried grasses (pampas grass), decorative branches, preserved (glycerined) eucalyptus, reindeer moss, Spanish moss

exclusions.condition: Vegetable bulbs, roots and corms primarily for FOOD use — onions, shallots, garlic, potatoes, sweet potatoes, yams
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 7 (edible vegetables) — onion sets for planting → 07.03 (not Ch.06). Seed potatoes → 07.01. The test: is the primary commercial use FOOD or ORNAMENTAL? Food-use bulbs/tubers → Ch.07. Ornamental tulip/daffodil bulbs → Ch.06

exclusions.condition: Bamboo, rattan and other plants primarily used as raw plaiting material (in rough form)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 14 (vegetable plaiting materials) heading 14.01 — raw bamboo for baskets/furniture, osier/willow for wickerwork → 14.01. Ornamental bamboo as a potted plant → 06.02

exclusions.condition: Chicory roots used for inulin/chicory extract production (industrial/food ingredient use)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 12.12 — industrial chicory roots (for inulin, chicory coffee substitute) → 12.12. Chicory plant/leaves as vegetable → Ch.07. Heading 06.01 specifically excludes 'roots of heading 12.12'

exclusions.condition: Artificial flowers, leaves and fruits made of plastic, paper, feathers or other materials
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 67.02 (artificial flowers/foliage/fruit and parts thereof; articles made from artificial flowers). Silk flower arrangements → 67.02. Plastic Christmas tree branches → 67.02. Only NATURAL plant materials → Ch.06

exclusions.condition: Pressed botanical specimens, herbarium collections, botanical collections for science
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 97.05 (collections and collectors' pieces of zoological, botanical, mineralogical, anatomical, historical or archaeological interest). Scientific herbarium specimens → 97.05

classification_rules.rule: 06.01 (propagation material: bulbs/tubers) vs 07.xx (food bulbs/tubers): ornamental vs food use intent
classification_rules.description: The key boundary for bulbs/tubers between Ch.06 and Ch.07 is the COMMERCIAL PURPOSE: ORNAMENTAL/HORTICULTURAL (tulip bulbs, daffodil bulbs, hyacinth, dahlia) → 06.01. FOOD PRODUCTION (onion sets for food growing, seed garlic, seed potatoes, yam tubers for food) → Ch.07. DUAL-USE plants: chicory plants/roots for ornamental → 06.01; chicory roots for industrial inulin → 12.12; chicory leaves as vegetable → Ch.07. The declared commercial purpose and typical trade characterisation determines the chapter for ambiguous cases.

classification_rules.rule: 06.02 (live plants/cuttings) vs 12.11 (plants for pharmacy/perfumery/insecticidal use): end use
classification_rules.description: Live plants in active growth → 06.02 regardless of species, including medicinal plants growing (e.g., pot of aloe vera, live lavender plant). Plants CUT OR DRIED and being traded as PHARMACEUTICAL/PERFUMERY/BOTANICAL RAW MATERIALS (lavender flowers, chamomile flowers, dried officinal herbs) → 12.11 or Ch.09. Growing plant traded as horticultural product → 06.02. The living/growing state generally confirms Ch.06 regardless of ultimate end use.

classification_rules.rule: Cut flowers and plant materials: fresh vs dyed/dried vs artificial
classification_rules.description: Three categories of flower/foliage products: (1) Ch.06 (06.03/06.04) = NATURAL plant materials in any preservation state FOR ORNAMENTAL USE: fresh, chilled, frozen, dried, bleached, dyed, impregnated with preservative (glycerin), silicone-coated — but must be made from REAL PLANT MATERIAL. (2) Ch.67 (67.02) = ARTIFICIAL flowers/foliage made from any material (plastic, paper, feathers, fabric, shell). (3) Food-dried plant parts (dried herbs/spices for consumption → Ch.09). The 'natural plant material + ornamental/decorative purpose' is Ch.06's domain.

classification_rules.rule: Mushroom spawn (06.02) vs live mushrooms for food (07.09) vs dried mushrooms (07.12)
classification_rules.description: Mushrooms appear in different chapters by development stage and use: MUSHROOM SPAWN (mycelium growing on growth medium — grain spawn, block spawn, dowel spawn — sold to growers for mushroom cultivation) → 06.02 (treated as planting material). FRESH/CHILLED MUSHROOMS for food → 07.09 (07.09.51 for cultivated; 07.09.59 for wild). DRIED MUSHROOMS for food → 07.12. MUSHROOM EXTRACT (for pharmaceutical/flavour) → Ch.21 or Ch.13.

classification_rules.rule: 06.04 (decorative foliage/branches) vs Ch.14 (plaiting material) vs Ch.44 (wood/bamboo): form and use
classification_rules.description: Foliage and branches at the boundary: ORNAMENTAL FOLIAGE in spray/bouquet form for florists (eucalyptus, ruscus, pittosporum, conifer branches for wreaths) → 06.04. DRIED GRASSES (pampas, millet, wheat decorative) → 06.04 if sold as ornamental floristry item. PLAITING MATERIAL (bamboo splits, osier, rattan — sold as raw wickerwork material) → Ch.14. CHRISTMAS TREES (conifer trees live or cut, temporarily imported for holiday season) → 06.02 (live trees) or 06.04 (cut branches). IMITATION/ARTIFICIAL Christmas tree → usually Ch.95 (festive article) or Ch.67. Real cut conifer Christmas tree → 06.04.
